visa_types.name: Student Visa : Student Visa Up To 90 Days
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kaz/en/che/travel-insurance)
Please note that the visa category chosen by the applicant must be based on the purpose of travel to Switzerland and the decision must be made by the applicant him/herself. An application
                                                                        filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa application. The applicant may need to submit additional details. The applicant may be asked to come for an interview
                                                                        at the Embassy. 

[Table:  Student Visa : Student Visa Up To 90 Days]
visa_types.visa_fees:
To submit your visa application, you will have to pay a visa fee and a service fee. The visa fee is collected on behalf of the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and the service
                                                                            fee is collected for the processing of your visa application at the VFS Global Visa Center. Visa fee for the application processing is 90 euro (the payment of visa fee accepts only in KZT 49 500). Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years –45 euros (payment accepts in KZT 25 000). Citizens of the countries (Belarus, Moldova, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Montenegro and Serbia)) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 35 euros (payment accepts in KZT 19 500)
                                                                        

[Table:  Student Visa : Student Visa Up To 90 Days]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in Tenge (KZT). The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and is updated
                                                                        periodically, therefore the Tenge amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above. Please note that your visa application fee receipt will only quote the fee paid
                                                                        in Euros.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee in the amount of 14 540 KZT(VAT 16% included)
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center in cash and by card only in KZT currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges. The visa and service fee, which is
                                                                        only collectible in KZT, may change as a result of currency fluctuation.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.
visa_types.processing_time: Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for study purposes and will stay no longer than 90 days please apply for the student short term visa category visa. You will have to provide
                                                                        a confirmation/matriculation letter from a School or university in Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Romania, Bulgaria, Croatia  and Switzerland.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for study purposes and will stay no longer than 90 days please apply for the student short term visa category visa. You will have to provide
                                                                        a confirmation/matriculation letter from a School or university in Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Romania, Bulgaria, Croatia  and Switzerland.

## Visa Fees
To submit your visa application, you will have to pay a visa fee and a service fee. The visa fee is collected on behalf of the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and the service
                                                                            fee is collected for the processing of your visa application at the VFS Global Visa Center. Visa fee for the application processing is 90 euro (the payment of visa fee accepts only in KZT 49 500). Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years –45 euros (payment accepts in KZT 25 000). Citizens of the countries (Belarus, Moldova, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Montenegro and Serbia)) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 35 euros (payment accepts in KZT 19 500)
                                                                        

[Table:  Student Visa : Student Visa Up To 90 Days]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in Tenge (KZT). The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and is updated
                                                                        periodically, therefore the Tenge amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above. Please note that your visa application fee receipt will only quote the fee paid
                                                                        in Euros.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee in the amount of 14 540 KZT(VAT 16% included)
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center in cash and by card only in KZT currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges. The visa and service fee, which is
                                                                        only collectible in KZT, may change as a result of currency fluctuation.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kaz/en/che/travel-insurance)
Please note that the visa category chosen by the applicant must be based on the purpose of travel to Switzerland and the decision must be made by the applicant him/herself. An application
                                                                        filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa application. The applicant may need to submit additional details. The applicant may be asked to come for an interview
                                                                        at the Embassy. 

[Table:  Student Visa : Student Visa Up To 90 Days]

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below. 
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It
                                                                            is preferable that ears be exposed.

## Travel Medical
- Travel Medical Insurance should cover the Schengen area with an amount of min. EUR 30’000.00 for the whole period of the whole travel (or in case of a two entry visa both travels). For multiple visas: the first travel has to be covered (the relevant field on the last page of the application form has to be signed).

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.

visa_types.name: Long Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein and will stay longer than 90 days, you should apply at the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana.
The below visa categories would fall under the long stay visa category if your stay is for more than 90 days.
- Student visa (long stay)
- Student Visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for study purposes and will stay longer than 90 days.
- Work visa (long stay)
- Work visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for work purposes and will stay more than 90 days.
- Take-up residence
- Take-up residence
You would like to apply for a Take-up residence visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
- Family reunion
- Family reunion
You would like to apply for a Family Reunion visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
visa_types.full_text:
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein and will stay longer than 90 days, you should apply at the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana.
The below visa categories would fall under the long stay visa category if your stay is for more than 90 days.
- Student visa (long stay)
- Student Visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for study purposes and will stay longer than 90 days.
- Work visa (long stay)
- Work visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for work purposes and will stay more than 90 days.
- Take-up residence
- Take-up residence
You would like to apply for a Take-up residence visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
- Family reunion
- Family reunion
You would like to apply for a Family Reunion visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.

                                                                        provide an invitation letter from the inviting organization in Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Citizens of the countries (Belarus, Moldova, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Montenegro and Serbia)) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 35 euros (payment accepts in KZT 19 500)
                                                                        

[Table:  Medical: Short Stay Medical Visit (Up To 90 Days)]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in Tenge (KZT). The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and is updated
                                                                        periodically, therefore the Tenge amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above. Please note that your visa application fee receipt will only quote the fee paid
                                                                        in Euros.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee in the amount of 14 540 KZT(VAT 16% included)
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center in cash and by card only in KZT currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges. The visa and service fee, which is
                                                                        only collectible in KZT, may change as a result of currency fluctuation.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for medical treatment purposes and will stay no longer than 90 days please apply under the medical visit visa category. You will have
                                                                        to provide a letter of invitation from a clinic in Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Romania, Bulgaria, Croatia and Switzerland.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for medical treatment purposes and will stay no longer than 90 days please apply under the medical visit visa category. You will have
                                                                        to provide a letter of invitation from a clinic in Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Romania, Bulgaria, Croatia and Switzerland.

## Visa Fees
To submit your visa application, you will have to pay a visa fee and a service fee. The visa fee is collected on behalf of the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and the service
                                                                            fee is collected for the processing of your visa application at the VFS Global Visa Center. Visa fee for the application processing is 90 euro (the payment of visa fee accepts only in KZT 49 500). Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years –45 euros (payment accepts in KZT 25 000). Citizens of the countries (Belarus, Moldova, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Montenegro and Serbia)) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 35 euros (payment accepts in KZT 19 500)
                                                                        

[Table:  Medical: Short Stay Medical Visit (Up To 90 Days)]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in Tenge (KZT). The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and is updated
                                                                        periodically, therefore the Tenge amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above. Please note that your visa application fee receipt will only quote the fee paid
                                                                        in Euros.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee in the amount of 14 540 KZT(VAT 16% included)
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center in cash and by card only in KZT currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges. The visa and service fee, which is
                                                                        only collectible in KZT, may change as a result of currency fluctuation.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kaz/en/che/travel-insurance)
Please note that the visa category chosen by the applicant must be based on the purpose of travel to Switzerland and the decision must be made by the applicant him/herself. An application
                                                                        filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa application. The applicant may need to submit additional details. The applicant may be asked to come for an interview
                                                                        at the Embassy. 

[Table:  Medical: Short Stay Medical Visit (Up To 90 Days)]

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below. 
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It
                                                                            is preferable that ears be exposed.

## Travel Medical
- Travel Medical Insurance should cover the Schengen area with an amount of min. EUR 30’000.00 for the whole period of the whole travel (or in case of a two entry visa both travels). For multiple visas: the first travel has to be covered (the relevant field on the last page of the application form has to be signed).

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.

[Table:  Visiting Family and Friends]

For multiple visas: the first travel has to be covered (the relevant field on the last page of the application form has to be signed).

## Overview
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein to participate to a sporting event, and will stay no longer than 90 days, please apply under this visa category. You should provide an
                                                                        invitation letter from the inviting organization in Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Romania, Bulgaria, Croatia and Switzerland.

## Visa Fees
To submit your visa application, you will have to pay a visa fee and a service fee. The visa fee is collected on behalf of the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and the service
                                                                            fee is collected for the processing of your visa application at the VFS Global Visa Center. Visa fee for the application processing is 90 euro (the payment of visa fee accepts only in KZT 49 500). Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years –45 euros (payment accepts in KZT 25 000). Citizens of the countries (Belarus, Moldova, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Montenegro and Serbia)) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 35 euros (payment accepts in KZT 19 500)
                                                                        

[Table:  Sport Visit]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in Tenge (KZT). The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana and is updated
                                                                        periodically, therefore the Tenge amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above. Please note that your visa application fee receipt will only quote the fee paid
                                                                        in Euros.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee in the amount of 14 540 KZT(VAT 16% included)
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center in cash and by card only in KZT currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges. The visa and service fee, which is
                                                                        only collectible in KZT, may change as a result of currency fluctuation.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kaz/en/che/travel-insurance)
Please note that the visa category chosen by the applicant must be based on the purpose of travel to Switzerland and the decision must be made by the applicant him/herself. An application
                                                                        filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa application. The applicant may need to submit additional details. The applicant may be asked to come for an interview
                                                                        at the Embassy. 

[Table:  Sport Visit]

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below. 
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It
                                                                            is preferable that ears be exposed.

## Travel Medical
- Travel Medical Insurance should cover the Schengen area with an amount of min. EUR 30’000.00 for the whole period of the whole travel (or in case of a two entry visa both travels). For multiple visas: the first travel has to be covered (the relevant field on the last page of the application form has to be signed).

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.

visa_types.name: SHORT STAY VISA
visa_types.overview:
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein and will stay no longer than 90 days. You can apply for a short stay visa.
VFS Global employees can provide you with information and application forms for Switzerland visas, but they are not able to provide you with advice regarding which visa you should apply for.
Before applying, please ensure you are very clear on your ‘purpose of visit’ – do remember we are here to assist and help you through the entire visa application process but are not permitted to advise or guide you on choosing a visa type. Since our work is primarily administrative in nature, we have no say on whether you will be granted a visa and how long it will take to process, as this is entirely the prerogative of the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana.

Before applying, please ensure you are very clear on your ‘purpose of visit’ – do remember we are here to assist and help you through the entire visa application process but are not permitted to advise or guide you on choosing a visa type. Since our work is primarily administrative in nature, we have no say on whether you will be granted a visa and how long it will take to process, as this is entirely the prerogative of the Embassy of Switzerland in Astana.
